title: urophyllum enneandrum
intro: Urophyllum enneandrum, also known as the 'Nine-Drum Urophyllum', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a small, evergreen shrub native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, valued for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Urophyllum enneandrum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory and skin problems.
